content: Curiosity-driven autonomous ICM agents can treat each new environment they explore as an online meta-learning task, using catastrophic forgetting mitigation mechanisms to consolidate only high-curiosity, information-dense memories rather than preserving all experienced transitions, leading to more sample-efficient lifelong exploration. This targeted memory consolidation frees the agent's working memory capacity to prioritize encoding novel, uncertain patterns that drive further intrinsic motivation, rather than filling storage with low-value redundant observations.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
